frameworks.name: domain-generality vs domain-specificity of metacognition
frameworks.one_line: tests whether self-evaluation relies on shared or task-specific neural resources

frameworks.name: multivoxel pattern analysis of confidence
frameworks.one_line: fMRI MVPA reveals confidence-predictive activity patterns across domains

frameworks.name: prefrontal substrate of metacognitive judgment
frameworks.one_line: anterior PFC implicated as neural locus for evaluating one's own cognition

core_question: Does metacognition rely on a domain-general resource or on domain-specific self-evaluative processes?
key_assumptions: metacognitive judgments can be elicited across perception and memory tasks
stimulus and performance can be matched across task domains
fMRI activity patterns can index confidence and accuracy
manipulations: matched stimulus and performance characteristics across domains
key_findings: multivoxel patterns in anterior prefrontal cortex predicted confidence in a domain-specific manner
domain-general signals predicting confidence and accuracy were found in frontal and posterior midline regions
both domain-specific and domain-general metacognitive representations coexist
interpretations: metacognition draws on both domain-general and domain-specific neural substrates
anterior PFC contributes domain-specific confidence representations while a wider network supports general self-evaluation
